rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed the unsafe strcpy at line 11 copying argc[1] into buf and explicitly called it a global buffer overflow from command-line input that can be arbitrarily long. They named the issue as a global buffer overflow.
rationale.local_fix: They suggested strncpy with sizeof(buf)-1 and ensuring null termination but did not propose rejecting overlong argv input before copying. The rubric requires rejecting overlong input in addition to bounded copy and null-termination.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended replacing unsafe string functions with bounded APIs and enforcing input validation at the boundary, which removes raw unbounded handling from the data path. This matches the root-cause/global remediation requirement.
